LAW AND MOTION 302, DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS HARVEY WRIGHT AND JENNIFER MAURUS' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE EIGHTH CAUSE OF ACTION AND OVERRULED AS TO THE SIXTH, NINTH, TENTH, ELEVENTH, AND TWELFTH CAUSES OF ACTION. AS TO THE EIGHTH CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF IS GIVEN LEAVE TO AMEND TO PLEAD THE GROUNDS UPON WHICH THE CAUSE OF ACTION IS BASED. THE REMAINING CAUSES OF ACTION ARE PROPERLY PLED. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; COURT REPORTER: LORETTA NAJERA CSR # 5153. (302)

MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF PAUL MITCHELL IN THE RIGHT OF AND FOR THE BENEFIT OF HELICAL PLANE, INC'S MOTION FOR DISQUALIFICATION OF COLT/WALLERSTEIN.
No appearances. This matter is taken off calendar. A Notice of Stay of Proceedings was filed June 8, 2012. (302/HEK) Judge: Harold Kahn; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR #5976

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-08-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT HELICAL PLANE, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is Sustained with ten day leave to amend as to the eighth cause of action and overruled as to the sixth, ninth, tenth, eleventh, and twelfth causes of action. As to the eighth cause of action, Plaintiff is given leave to amend to plead the grounds upon which the cause of action is based. The remaining causes of action are adequately pled. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Court Reporter: Loretta Najera CSR # 5153

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-08-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS HARVEY WRIGHT AND JENNIFER MAURUS' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is Sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the eighth cause of action and overruled as to the sixth, ninth, tenth, eleventh, and twelfth causes of action. As to the eighth cause of action, Plaintiff is given leave to amend to plead the grounds upon which the cause of action is based. The remaining causes of action are properly pled. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Court Reporter: Loretta Najera CSR # 5153
